intro.uz: Chempion — gʻalaba qozongan emas, balki toʻgʻri va yaxshi narsa uchun kurashadigan inson. Seth Godin aytadi: oddiy boʻlma, ajralib tur. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi shu gʻoyaga xizmat qiladi: qiyoslashning yuqori shakllari (by far / one of the most / the best … ever) va oʻtmish xulosasi (must have / can't have + V3). Inca haqidagi POV hikoyada ularni his qilasiz.
intro.en: A champion is not just a winner — it's someone who fights for what is right and good. Seth Godin says: don't be normal, be remarkable. This week's two grammar topics serve that idea: advanced comparatives and superlatives (by far / one of the most / the best … ever) and past deduction (must have / can't have + V3). You'll feel both in the POV story about Inca.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida chempionni taʼriflab, uni kuchli tildagi qiyoslash bilan ustun qoʻya olasiz (by far the best; one of the most … ; the best … ever) va oʻtmishdagi voqeaga mantiqiy xulosa chiqara olasiz (She must have …; They can't have …).
canDo.en: By the end you can describe a champion and rank them with strong comparisons (by far the best; one of the most …; the best … ever), and make logical deductions about the past (She must have …; They can't have …).

grammar.titleUz: Qiyoslashning yuqori shakllari: by far / one of the most / the best … ever
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — emphasise and rank with by far / one of the most / the best … ever
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can intensify comparisons (by far, one of the most, the best … ever)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha superlativ bitta soʻz — “eng” bilan yasaladi (eng zoʻr), qiyosiy esa “-roq” bilan (zoʻrroq). Ingliz tili qisqa sifatga -est, uzun sifatga most qoʻshadi va oldiga majburiy “the” qoʻyadi (the greatest). “eng … laridan biri” aynan “one of the most + koʻplik ot” boʻladi, va “ancha, beqiyos” = by far.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ She is one of the best writer. He is the most great. ✅ She is one of the best writers. He is the greatest. “one of the …” dan keyin ot KOʻPLIKda; qisqa sifatda “most” emas, -est (great → greatest).

examples.en: Being a champion is by far the best choice for your career.
examples.uz: Chempion boʻlish — karyerangiz uchun ancha zoʻr tanlov.

examples.en: Seth Godin is one of the most respected writers in marketing.
examples.uz: Seth Godin — marketingdagi eng hurmatli yozuvchilardan biri.

examples.en: That was the greatest risk Inca had ever taken.
examples.uz: Bu — Inca qilgan eng katta tavakkal edi.

exercises.prompt: Champions are ___ the most valuable people in any company.
exercises.hintUz: farqni kuchaytirish (ancha, beqiyos) → by far + superlativ

exercises.prompt: She is one of the most creative ___ I know.
exercises.hintUz: one of the most … dan keyin ot KOʻPLIKda: people (person emas)

exercises.prompt: It was the best decision I have ___ made.
exercises.hintUz: the best … ever / the best … I have ever + V3 — tajribadagi eng yuqori nuqta

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz sevgan narsa haqida uchta jumla ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “… is by far the best … . It is one of the most … . It is the best … I have ever … .”
exercises.hintUz: by far the best; one of the most + koʻplik; the best … ever

grammar.titleUz: Oʻtmish xulosasi: must have / can't have + V3
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking & Writing — explain past events with logical deduction
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can make deductions about the past (must / might / can't have + V3)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada bitta ibora — “-gan boʻlsa kerak / boʻlishi mumkin” — barcha darajani beradi. Ingliz tili esa ishonch darajasiga qarab uch xil modalni V3 bilan ishlatadi: must have (aminman), might/may have (ehtimol), can't have (mumkin emas). L05ʼdagi hozirgi xulosa (must be) endi oʻtmishga koʻchadi (must have been).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ She must worked hard. He mustn't have known. ✅ She must have worked hard. He can't have known. “have” tushib qolmasin (modal + have + V3); inkor xulosa uchun mustn't have EMAS, can't have.

examples.en: Inca must have believed in her idea.
examples.uz: Inca oʻz gʻoyasiga ishongan boʻlsa kerak.

examples.en: The rich people can't have liked the plan at first.
examples.uz: Boylar rejani boshida yoqtirgan boʻlishi mumkin emas.

examples.en: She might have felt alone at times.
examples.uz: U baʼzida oʻzini yolgʻiz his qilgan boʻlishi mumkin.

exercises.prompt: The room is empty — everyone ___ home already.
exercises.hintUz: dalilga qarab aniq xulosa → must have + V3 (gone)

exercises.prompt: She trained every single day, so she ___ lazy.
exercises.hintUz: inkor xulosa (mumkin emas) → can't have + V3 (mustn't have emas)

exercises.prompt: He didn't answer the phone — he ___ asleep.

exercises.promptUz: POVdagi Inca haqida uchta xulosa ayting: “She must have … . She might have … . People can't have … .”

vocab.defEn: a person who fights for or defends what is good
vocab.example: Inca was a champion for poor people.

vocab.defEn: to fight for or defend something good
vocab.example: Champion an innovation at your job.

vocab.defEn: a sudden, big improvement or new idea
vocab.example: Building houses from plastic bottles was a great innovation.

vocab.uz: chorlamoq, chaqiriq tashlamoq, jurʼat qilishga undamoq
vocab.defEn: to challenge or encourage someone to do something bold
vocab.example: I dare you to do something great.

vocab.uz: hozirgi holat, anʼanaviy tartib, eski yoʻl
vocab.defEn: the current, traditional situation; the normal old way
vocab.example: Being a champion is about overcoming the status quo.

vocab.defEn: to fight against and move past the traditional way
vocab.example: Success means overcoming the status quo and inertia.

vocab.defEn: resistance to change or to starting to move
vocab.example: People have inertia; they resist change at first.

vocab.uz: quvonch bilan qabul qilmoq, quchoq ochib kutmoq
vocab.defEn: to accept something happily and fully
vocab.example: The rich people finally embraced her idea.
Will you be remarkable, or careful and normal?

vocab.defEn: only average; not good and not bad
vocab.example: Don't be careful, normal and mediocre.

englishpod.title: The Office — Interview Skills: Describing Yourself

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Parsons
dialogue.en: Okay Rebecca. Now youyou’ve given me a good idea of your work and academic background, but what about you as a person? How would you describe your key strengths?

dialogue.speaker: Rebecca
dialogue.en: Well Mr. Parsons, as I mentioned before I’m someone who needs new challenges. I’m really focused and hard hard-working. I think my academic results prove this.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Parsons
dialogue.en: Yes, true, but how about other personal qualities?

dialogue.speaker: Rebecca
dialogue.en: Well I believe I’m a good team-player, but I can also work well independently. I’m very enthusiastic and , well I hope my colleagues would agree, I’m fun to work with.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Parsons
dialogue.en: What would you say is your most positive quality?

dialogue.speaker: Rebecca
dialogue.en: Hmmmmm, that’s a tough question. But I would have to say my passion. I’m really passionate about journalism and passionate about my career.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Rebecca ish intervyusida oʻz kuchli tomonlarini sanaydi: “fokuslangan, mehnatkash, ishtiyoqli.” Chempion boʻlish shu yerdan boshlanadi — oddiy emas, ajralib turadigan qilib oʻzingni tanitish. Sizningcha, Rebecca ishga olinadimi? Oʻzingizni bitta jumla bilan qanday taʼriflaysiz?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Rebecca describes her strengths in a job interview: focused, hardworking, passionate. Being a champion starts here — presenting yourself as remarkable, not ordinary. Do you think Rebecca will get the job? How would you describe yourself in one sentence?

keyVocab.defEn: the qualities that make someone effective or useful
giving full attention and effort to a specific task
putting a lot of effort into your work
to show the truth of something with evidence
a person who works well together with a group
having a lot of interest in or passion for something
the strong enthusiasm you feel when doing something

sixmin.title: How excited are you about the World Cup?

quiz.q: The Russia 2018 mascot is a wolf called Zabivaka. What does his name mean in English?
quiz.qUz: Rossiya-2018 timsoli — Zabivaka ismli boʻri. Uning ismi inglizchada nimani anglatadi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) The one who scores (gol uradigan). Neil a) The winner dedi va xato qildi — Rob oxirida javobni ochdi: “The answer is b) The one who scores.”

vocab.defEn: among the best in the world

vocab.uz: gʻalabaga umid qilmoq, imkoni bor deb hisoblamoq
vocab.defEn: to think you are likely to succeed, though you might not
to support someone, e.g. a team

vocab.uz: gʻalaba ehtimoli kam tomon, kuchsizroq raqib
vocab.defEn: the team or person considered least likely to win

vocab.uz: sportchilik axloqi, halol va adolatli xatti-harakat
vocab.defEn: fair and honest behaviour in a game or sport
a lot of talk and publicity to make people interested in something

funEnglish.title: How to develop a mindset for peak performance | Marc Cormier | TEDxUKY

speakingPrompt.en: Think of a real champion you admire — someone who fought for what is right. Why are they by far the best, or one of the most remarkable people you know? Looking back, what must they have done, and what challenges can't have been easy for them?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: qiyoslashning yuqori shakllari (by far the best; one of the most …; the best … ever) va oʻtmish xulosasi (She must have …; It can't have been easy …).
